Test Title: Login with valid email and password
Objective: Verify that a registered user can log in successfully and enter the main application flow.
Preconditions: A valid user account exists in Firebase and the backend auth service is reachable.
Test Steps: Step 1: Launch the app. | Step 2: Open Login screen. | Step 3: Enter a valid email. | Step 4: Enter the correct password. | Step 5: Tap Sign In.
Expected Result: User is authenticated successfully, redirected to the main application area, and the app session is created without errors.
Postconditions: User remains logged in until logout or session expiry.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: User is authenticated successfully, redirected to the main application area, and the app session is created without errors.

Test Title: Login with invalid password
Objective: Verify that the system rejects incorrect credentials and shows a proper error message.
Preconditions: A valid user account exists.
Test Steps: Step 1: Launch the app. | Step 2: Open Login screen. | Step 3: Enter a valid email. | Step 4: Enter an incorrect password. | Step 5: Tap Sign In.
Expected Result: Login is blocked, the user stays on the Login screen, and a clear authentication error message is displayed.
Postconditions: No session is created.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Login is blocked, the user stays on the Login screen, and a clear authentication error message is displayed.

Test Title: Register a new account with valid details
Objective: Verify that a new user can create an account using valid registration details.
Preconditions: The email address used for registration is not already associated with an account.
Test Data: Valid name; new email address; strong password.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Register screen. | Step 2: Enter a valid full name. | Step 3: Enter a new email address. | Step 4: Enter a valid password. | Step 5: Submit the registration form.
Expected Result: The account is created successfully, verification flow is triggered if configured, and the user can proceed into the app authentication flow.
Postconditions: A new user account exists in the authentication system.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The account is created successfully, verification flow is triggered if configured, and the user can proceed into the app authentication flow.

Test Title: Register with an existing email address
Objective: Verify that the app prevents duplicate account registration for an already used email address.
Preconditions: An account already exists for the test email.
Test Data: Existing email address; valid name; valid password.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Register screen. | Step 2: Enter a valid name. | Step 3: Enter an email that is already registered. | Step 4: Enter a valid password. | Step 5: Submit the form.
Expected Result: Registration is rejected and the user sees a duplicate email or already registered message.
Postconditions: No additional account is created.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Registration is rejected and the user sees a duplicate email or already registered message.

Test Title: Send password reset request
Objective: Verify that the forgot password flow accepts a valid email and confirms the request.
Preconditions: A valid user account exists with the provided email address.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Forgot Password screen. | Step 2: Enter a valid registered email. | Step 3: Submit the reset request.
Expected Result: The app confirms that the password reset request was sent successfully.
Postconditions: A password reset request is recorded by the auth provider or backend.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app confirms that the password reset request was sent successfully.

Test Title: Logout from the application
Objective: Verify that an authenticated user can log out and that protected app areas are no longer accessible.
Preconditions: The user is already logged in.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app. | Step 2: Open Profile or Settings. | Step 3: Tap Logout.
Expected Result: The user is signed out, returned to the auth flow, and main application screens are no longer accessible without logging in again.
Postconditions: Session tokens and user state are cleared according to app behavior.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The user is signed out, returned to the auth flow, and main application screens are no longer accessible without logging in again.

Test Title: Load home dashboard content successfully
Objective: Verify that the home dashboard loads profile data, recipes, and seasonal content after login.
Preconditions: User is logged in and the backend services are reachable.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app. | Step 2: Wait for the Home screen to load completely.
Expected Result: Home screen loads without crash and displays key content areas such as user context, recommendations, seasonal items, or recipe data.
Postconditions: Home dashboard remains interactive.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Home screen loads without crash and displays key content areas such as user context, recommendations, seasonal items, or recipe data.

Test Title: Handle home screen API failure gracefully
Objective: Verify that the Home screen handles service errors without crashing the app.
Preconditions: User is logged in; backend API is temporarily unavailable or intentionally mocked to fail.
Test Data: Authenticated user account; unavailable API state.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app while the home-related API calls fail. | Step 2: Observe the Home screen state.
Expected Result: The app remains stable and shows an appropriate fallback, loading, or error state instead of crashing.
Postconditions: User can continue using the app or retry loading.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API unavailable or mocked failure.
Actual Result: The app remains stable and shows an appropriate fallback, loading, or error state instead of crashing.

Test Title: Search recipes from a food memory prompt
Objective: Verify that the Memory feature accepts a valid food memory prompt and returns similar dishes.
Preconditions: Memory-related API endpoint is reachable.
Test Data: Memory prompt such as childhood coconut milk curry with rice.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the Memory tab. | Step 2: Enter a descriptive food memory prompt. | Step 3: Submit the search.
Expected Result: The system returns suggested dishes related to the prompt and displays them on the suggestions screen.
Postconditions: A memory search session is created for the user flow.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend AI or memory service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The system returns suggested dishes related to the prompt and displays them on the suggestions screen.

Test Title: Validate empty memory prompt
Objective: Verify that the Memory feature does not allow an empty memory prompt to be submitted.
Preconditions: Memory screen is available.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the Memory screen. | Step 2: Leave the input empty. | Step 3: Tap the search or continue action.
Expected Result: Validation is shown, no request is sent, and the user remains on the Memory screen.
Postconditions: No memory search request is created.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend available or not required.
Actual Result: Validation is shown, no request is sent, and the user remains on the Memory screen.

Test Title: Open recipe customization from a selected memory suggestion
Objective: Verify that tapping a suggested dish opens the recipe customization flow for that dish.
Preconditions: A list of suggested dishes has already been returned from a memory search.
Test Data: Any suggestion from the results list.
Test Steps: Step 1: Perform a successful memory search. | Step 2: On the Similar Dishes screen, tap one suggested dish.
Expected Result: Recipe customization opens for the selected dish without navigation errors.
Postconditions: Selected dish context is carried into the next screen.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend AI or memory service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Recipe customization opens for the selected dish without navigation errors.

Test Title: Load AI-generated recipe content for a generated dish
Objective: Verify that the app can load or generate recipe content when the selected dish does not map directly to a stored recipe ID.
Preconditions: An AI-generated or non-standard dish identifier is available from the memory flow.
Test Data: Dish identifier similar to ai:kiri-hodi or generated name.
Test Steps: Step 1: Enter the recipe customization flow with an AI-generated dish identifier. | Step 2: Wait for recipe content to load.
Expected Result: The app generates or retrieves recipe details successfully and displays ingredients and instructions instead of failing on an invalid database lookup.
Postconditions: Generated recipe is available for further actions such as scaling or cooking.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend AI generation service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app generates or retrieves recipe details successfully and displays ingredients and instructions instead of failing on an invalid database lookup.

Test Title: Scale servings from the memory recipe flow
Objective: Verify that changing the serving size updates quantities and time-related recipe details.
Preconditions: Recipe customization screen is open with recipe content loaded.
Test Data: Recipe with ingredient quantities and serving controls.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Recipe Customization from the Memory flow. | Step 2: Increase or decrease the serving size. | Step 3: Observe the recalculated values.
Expected Result: Ingredient quantities and any displayed cooking-time values update consistently to match the new serving size.
Postconditions: Updated recipe values remain visible for the cooking session.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend scaling endpoint available if required; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Ingredient quantities and any displayed cooking-time values update consistently to match the new serving size.

Test Title: Apply local substitution in recipe customization
Objective: Verify that the user can use local ingredient adaptation suggestions inside the recipe customization flow.
Preconditions: Recipe customization screen is open and the recipe contains ingredients eligible for local substitution.
Test Data: Recipe with at least one substitutable ingredient.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Recipe Customization. | Step 2: Open local adaptation or substitution options. | Step 3: Select a suggested substitute.
Expected Result: Selected ingredient is replaced or updated in the recipe context and the user can continue cooking with the adapted version.
Postconditions: Adapted recipe remains usable in the cooking flow.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend local adaptation service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Selected ingredient is replaced or updated in the recipe context and the user can continue cooking with the adapted version.

Test Title: Track progress through cooking steps
Objective: Verify that the user can move through and mark cooking steps during the cooking flow.
Preconditions: Recipe instructions are available and the cooking flow has been started.
Test Steps: Step 1: Start cooking from the recipe customization screen. | Step 2: Open Cooking Steps. | Step 3: Mark steps as completed one by one.
Expected Result: Completed steps are visibly tracked and the user can continue through the recipe progression without losing state.
Postconditions: Cooking progress is retained during the active session.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend not required after recipe load.
Actual Result: Completed steps are visibly tracked and the user can continue through the recipe progression without losing state.

Test Title: Navigate from done screen to timer and then to feedback
Objective: Verify that the completion flow can continue into the timer and then open feedback.
Preconditions: A cooking session has been completed and the Done screen is reachable.
Test Steps: Step 1: Complete a cooking session. | Step 2: From the Done screen, start the timer. | Step 3: Allow the timer to finish.
Expected Result: The timer starts successfully and, on completion, the app opens the feedback screen.
Postconditions: Feedback screen is ready for submission.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend not required after recipe load.
Actual Result: The timer starts successfully and, on completion, the app opens the feedback screen.

Test Title: Submit cooking feedback successfully
Objective: Verify that the user can submit cooking feedback with rating and comments after finishing a recipe.
Preconditions: User has completed a cooking session and is on the Feedback screen.
Test Data: Rating value; optional comment; optional improvement notes.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Feedback screen after cooking. | Step 2: Enter a rating. | Step 3: Enter comments or tips if fields are available. | Step 4: Submit feedback.
Expected Result: Feedback is saved successfully and the app returns the user to the appropriate next screen or main flow.
Postconditions: Feedback record exists in the relevant storage path.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase or backend feedback service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Feedback is saved successfully and the app returns the user to the appropriate next screen or main flow.

Test Title: Search by exact dish name
Objective: Verify that the Search feature returns relevant results for a direct dish-name query.
Preconditions: Search API is reachable.
Test Data: Dish query such as chicken curry.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the Search tab. | Step 2: Enter an exact dish name. | Step 3: Submit the search.
Expected Result: Relevant recipe results are displayed for the searched dish.
Postconditions: Search history may be updated according to app behavior.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend search endpoint available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Relevant recipe results are displayed for the searched dish.

Test Title: Fallback to similar dishes for ingredient-intent search
Objective: Verify that the Search screen uses fallback behavior when the search intent is ingredient-based and direct results are empty.
Preconditions: Search API and similar-dishes API are reachable.
Test Data: Ingredient query such as jackfruit seed or curry leaf.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the Search screen. | Step 2: Enter an ingredient-focused query that returns no direct recipes. | Step 3: Submit the search.
Expected Result: The app shows fallback similar dishes, adaptations, or related results instead of a broken empty screen.
Postconditions: User can still continue exploring results.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend search and similar dishes endpoints available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app shows fallback similar dishes, adaptations, or related results instead of a broken empty screen.

Test Title: Store unique search history in recency order
Objective: Verify that repeated searches do not create duplicate history entries and that the latest query is moved to the top.
Preconditions: Search history feature is enabled through local storage.
Test Data: Queries such as kottu; lamprais; kottu again.
Test Steps: Step 1: Perform a search for the first query. | Step 2: Perform a search for the second query. | Step 3: Repeat the first query. | Step 4: Open search history.
Expected Result: Search history shows unique query entries only, with the most recent matching query at the top.
Postconditions: Local search history is updated.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Local storage available.
Actual Result: Search history shows unique query entries only, with the most recent matching query at the top.

Test Title: Clear or remove search history entries
Objective: Verify that the user can remove individual history entries or clear the entire search history.
Preconditions: Search history already contains one or more entries.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Search history. | Step 2: Remove one item or tap clear history if the feature is available. | Step 3: Reopen history.
Expected Result: Removed entries no longer appear and the history reflects the user's action immediately.
Postconditions: Search history storage reflects the updated list.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Local storage available.
Actual Result: Removed entries no longer appear and the history reflects the user's action immediately.

Test Title: Load seasonal ingredient suggestions
Objective: Verify that the seasonal food or local adaptation flow loads seasonal ingredients when the screen opens.
Preconditions: Seasonal API is reachable.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Local Adaptation or Seasonal Food screen. | Step 2: Wait for the initial content to load.
Expected Result: Seasonal ingredient suggestions or related content load successfully without crash.
Postconditions: Seasonal content remains interactive for further navigation.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Seasonal API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Seasonal ingredient suggestions or related content load successfully without crash.

Test Title: Search local substitutions for a dish or ingredient
Objective: Verify that the app returns local substitutions and related recipes for an adaptation query.
Preconditions: Adaptation-related APIs are reachable.
Test Data: Dish or ingredient query such as coconut milk or breadfruit curry.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Local Adaptation screen. | Step 2: Enter a dish or ingredient. | Step 3: Submit the search.
Expected Result: The app shows substitution suggestions, adaptation guidance, and any associated recipes returned by the service.
Postconditions: User can open additional details from the results.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Adaptation API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app shows substitution suggestions, adaptation guidance, and any associated recipes returned by the service.

Test Title: Open ingredient guide from an adaptation result
Objective: Verify that selecting an ingredient guide opens a detailed guide page for the ingredient.
Preconditions: Local adaptation results include an ingredient guide link or guide-capable result.
Test Data: Any result with a guide entry.
Test Steps: Step 1: Perform a successful adaptation search. | Step 2: Tap the Ingredient Guide option for one result.
Expected Result: Ingredient Guide screen opens and displays the expected ingredient details.
Postconditions: Guide content is available for review and navigation.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Ingredient guide API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Ingredient Guide screen opens and displays the expected ingredient details.

Test Title: Submit a new adaptation with image upload
Objective: Verify that a user can create a new adaptation entry and upload an image successfully.
Preconditions: User is logged in and the upload endpoint is available.
Test Data: Valid adaptation title; ingredient details; description; image file.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Add Adaptation screen. | Step 2: Fill in all required fields. | Step 3: Select an image. | Step 4: Submit the form.
Expected Result: The image uploads successfully and the adaptation is submitted without validation or network errors.
Postconditions: A new adaptation record exists or the app shows successful submission confirmation.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Upload API available; Adaptation submission API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The image uploads successfully and the adaptation is submitted without validation or network errors.

Test Title: Validate required fields on Add Adaptation
Objective: Verify that Add Adaptation does not submit when mandatory information is missing.
Preconditions: Add Adaptation screen is available.
Test Data: Missing title, missing ingredient, or missing description.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Add Adaptation screen. | Step 2: Leave one or more required fields blank. | Step 3: Tap Submit.
Expected Result: The app blocks submission and displays clear validation messages for the missing fields.
Postconditions: No adaptation is submitted.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend not required to verify local validation.
Actual Result: The app blocks submission and displays clear validation messages for the missing fields.

Test Title: Submit a new recipe from the Add Recipe screen
Objective: Verify that a user can create a recipe with all required data and upload an image if provided.
Preconditions: User is logged in; Firebase and upload services are available.
Test Data: Valid recipe title; ingredients; steps; optional image.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Add Recipe screen. | Step 2: Enter all required recipe information. | Step 3: Add an image if needed. | Step 4: Submit the recipe.
Expected Result: The recipe is created successfully and stored with the expected draft, pending, or submitted state defined by the app flow.
Postconditions: New recipe is available in the appropriate library or moderation path.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase and upload services available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The recipe is created successfully and stored with the expected draft, pending, or submitted state defined by the app flow.

Test Title: Validate required fields on Add Recipe
Objective: Verify that recipe creation is blocked when required fields such as title, ingredients, or instructions are incomplete.
Preconditions: Add Recipe screen is available.
Test Data: Missing recipe title; missing ingredient list; missing instructions.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Add Recipe screen. | Step 2: Leave required fields incomplete. | Step 3: Tap Submit.
Expected Result: The app prevents submission and displays the relevant validation messages.
Postconditions: No recipe is created.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend not required to verify local validation.
Actual Result: The app prevents submission and displays the relevant validation messages.

Test Title: Open scaled recipe results from a valid quantity query
Objective: Verify that the Smart Scaling flow accepts a valid ingredient-quantity query and loads scaled results.
Preconditions: Scaling API is reachable.
Test Data: Query such as I have 500g chicken and need curry for 4 people.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Smart Scaling screen. | Step 2: Enter a valid quantity-based query. | Step 3: Continue to the results screen.
Expected Result: The app navigates to Scaled Recipe Results and displays relevant scaled recipe suggestions.
Postconditions: User can inspect or select one of the suggested results.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Scaling API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app navigates to Scaled Recipe Results and displays relevant scaled recipe suggestions.

Test Title: Handle invalid or ambiguous scaling query safely
Objective: Verify that invalid scaling input does not crash the app and produces a safe error or fallback state.
Preconditions: Scaling screen is available.
Test Data: Ambiguous query such as some chicken for family.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Smart Scaling screen. | Step 2: Enter an unclear or invalid query. | Step 3: Submit the request.
Expected Result: The app stays stable and shows a meaningful error, empty state, or retry prompt rather than crashing.
Postconditions: User can correct the query and retry.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Scaling API available or mocked failure; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app stays stable and shows a meaningful error, empty state, or retry prompt rather than crashing.

Test Title: Open recipe customization from scaled results
Objective: Verify that tapping a scaled recipe suggestion opens the detailed customization screen with scaled data.
Preconditions: Scaled results are already visible on the Scaled Recipe Results screen.
Test Steps: Step 1: Perform a successful scaling search. | Step 2: Tap one result on the Scaled Recipe Results screen.
Expected Result: Recipe customization opens and shows the selected recipe with scaled quantities or context.
Postconditions: User can continue cooking or editing from the selected scaled recipe.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Scaling API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Recipe customization opens and shows the selected recipe with scaled quantities or context.

Test Title: Load digital cookbook content
Objective: Verify that the digital cookbook section loads user recipes, cookbooks, and saved items when Firebase-backed content is available.
Preconditions: User is logged in and Firebase is configured.
Test Data: Authenticated user with at least one recipe, cookbook, or saved item.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app. | Step 2: Open the Digital Cookbook or Library screen. | Step 3: Wait for content to load.
Expected Result: The screen loads successfully and displays the user's cookbook-related content without crash.
Postconditions: Library remains available for navigation to recipe or cookbook detail pages.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The screen loads successfully and displays the user's cookbook-related content without crash.

Test Title: Select recipes for a new cookbook
Objective: Verify that the user can select existing recipes for inclusion in a new cookbook.
Preconditions: User has at least one eligible recipe in their account.
Test Steps: Step 1: Start the cookbook creation flow. | Step 2: Open the recipe selection step. | Step 3: Select one or more recipes. | Step 4: Continue.
Expected Result: Selected recipes are retained and the workflow moves to the next cookbook setup step.
Postconditions: Selected recipe list remains available in the creation flow.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Selected recipes are retained and the workflow moves to the next cookbook setup step.

Test Title: Validate mandatory fields in cookbook cover setup
Objective: Verify that the cookbook cover setup step requires essential metadata before allowing the user to continue.
Preconditions: User is already in the cookbook creation flow.
Test Data: Missing title; missing author; valid cover data.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Cookbook Cover Setup screen. | Step 2: Leave title or author blank. | Step 3: Try to continue. | Step 4: Enter valid values and continue again.
Expected Result: The app blocks incomplete input, shows validation, and only proceeds when required fields are provided.
Postconditions: Cookbook setup data is stored for the next step after valid submission.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The app blocks incomplete input, shows validation, and only proceeds when required fields are provided.

Test Title: Publish a cookbook successfully from the summary step
Objective: Verify that the cookbook can be finalized and published or saved from the summary screen.
Preconditions: Cookbook creation data is complete through previous steps.
Test Data: Completed cookbook draft with selected recipes and metadata.
Test Steps: Step 1: Complete recipe selection and cover setup. | Step 2: Open the summary screen. | Step 3: Review the information. | Step 4: Submit or publish the cookbook.
Expected Result: Cookbook is saved successfully and the user receives confirmation or is navigated to the next appropriate screen.
Postconditions: New cookbook record exists in the relevant data store.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Cookbook is saved successfully and the user receives confirmation or is navigated to the next appropriate screen.

Test Title: Open cookbook reference content from the community module
Objective: Verify that the community cookbook introduction flow can open its reference material correctly.
Preconditions: Community flow is accessible from the app navigation.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the Community-related cookbook flow. | Step 2: Navigate from the introduction screen to the reference screen.
Expected Result: The reference screen opens successfully and displays the expected guidance content.
Postconditions: User can continue or return from the reference screen.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend not required if content is static.
Actual Result: The reference screen opens successfully and displays the expected guidance content.

Test Title: Display thank-you screen after a successful community submission
Objective: Verify that the community submission flow ends with a clear success confirmation screen.
Preconditions: Community submission flow is available and required data is complete.
Test Steps: Step 1: Complete the community submission flow. | Step 2: Submit the final step.
Expected Result: A thank-you or success confirmation screen is displayed to the user.
Postconditions: Submission flow ends cleanly.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend or Firebase available if submission is persisted.
Actual Result: A thank-you or success confirmation screen is displayed to the user.

Test Title: Load current user profile details
Objective: Verify that the profile screen displays the logged-in user's current account information.
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app. | Step 2: Open Profile Settings screen.
Expected Result: The screen displays the current user details such as name, email, and profile image if available.
Postconditions: Profile screen remains interactive.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase or stored user data available.
Actual Result: The screen displays the current user details such as name, email, and profile image if available.

Test Title: Update display name successfully
Objective: Verify that the user can change their display name and save the updated profile.
Preconditions: User is logged in and Edit Profile is accessible.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Edit Profile screen. | Step 2: Change the display name. | Step 3: Save the profile.
Expected Result: The updated display name is saved successfully and reflected on profile-related screens.
Postconditions: Updated profile data persists after refresh or reopening the screen.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase or backend profile update service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The updated display name is saved successfully and reflected on profile-related screens.

Test Title: Upload or replace profile photo
Objective: Verify that the user can add or replace a profile photo successfully.
Preconditions: User is logged in and image picker plus upload service are available.
Test Data: Valid image file from the device.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Edit Profile screen. | Step 2: Choose a profile photo from the image picker. | Step 3: Save the profile changes.
Expected Result: The selected image is uploaded successfully and the new profile photo is displayed.
Postconditions: Updated profile photo persists after refresh or reopening the app.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Image picker permission granted; Upload service available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The selected image is uploaded successfully and the new profile photo is displayed.

Test Title: Redirect user to login when session expires
Objective: Verify that the app handles expired or invalid sessions safely and returns the user to the auth flow.
Preconditions: A previously authenticated session exists and the token is expired or invalid.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open the app with an expired or invalid session. | Step 2: Navigate to a protected action or wait for an authenticated request.
Expected Result: The app detects the invalid session, clears the session state as needed, and redirects the user to the login or auth flow without crashing.
Postconditions: User is signed out and must log in again.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Backend available or mocked expired session.
Actual Result: The app detects the invalid session, clears the session state as needed, and redirects the user to the login or auth flow without crashing.

Test Title: Load notifications for the current user
Objective: Verify that the app can load and display notifications for the logged-in user.
Preconditions: User is logged in and has notification data available.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in to the app. | Step 2: Open the Notifications screen.
Expected Result: Notification list loads successfully and displays available notifications in a stable layout.
Postconditions: Notification list remains available for user review.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: Notification list loads successfully and displays available notifications in a stable layout.

Test Title: Persist notification preference changes
Objective: Verify that enabling or disabling notification settings is saved and still visible when the user reopens the settings screen.
Preconditions: User is logged in and Notification Settings screen is accessible.
Test Data: Toggle combinations for reminders, recommendations, or other available preferences.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Notification Settings. | Step 2: Change one or more preference toggles. | Step 3: Save if required. | Step 4: Close and reopen Notification Settings.
Expected Result: The selected notification preferences are saved successfully and the same values are shown when the settings screen is reopened.
Postconditions: Updated notification settings remain active for future app behavior.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase configured; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The selected notification preferences are saved successfully and the same values are shown when the settings screen is reopened.

Test Title: Remember Me preserves the saved email for the next login
Objective: Verify that enabling Remember Me stores the user's email and makes it available on the next login attempt.
Preconditions: Remember Me option is available and the user has valid credentials.
Test Data: Valid email; valid password; Remember Me enabled.
Test Steps: Step 1: Log in with Remember Me enabled. | Step 2: Log out. | Step 3: Reopen the app or return to Login.
Expected Result: The saved email remains available on the Login screen according to app behavior.
Postconditions: User is logged out but remembered email remains stored.
Environment: Android or iOS test device; Expo app build; Firebase test project; Backend API available; Stable internet.
Actual Result: The saved email remains available on the Login screen according to app behavior.

Test Title: Validate empty login form fields
Objective: Verify that login is blocked when email or password is left empty.
Preconditions: Login screen is available.
Test Steps: Step 1: Open Login screen. | Step 2: Leave email and password empty or fill only one field. | Step 3: Tap Sign In.
Expected Result: Validation is shown and no login request is processed.
